top_headline: AMD +18.6% best post-earnings day in 7 years; Brent -7.8% $101 on Iran peace MOU
chapters: I · MACRO — Warsh Vote Wk May 11 / ADP +109K vs 84K est / Jobless Claims + Productivity Today / NFP Friday
II · AI TRADE — AMD $10.25B +38% Q1 Record / DC $5.8B +57% / Q2 Guide $11.2B / NVDA +5.4% Lagging / ARM Record $1.49B
III · EARNINGS — Honor Roll: DIS+7%, UBER+10%, ARM Record, APP+59%, SHOP+34% / PLTR -9.7% Valuation Compression / Movers
IV · GEOPOLITICS — Iran Peace MOU via Pakistan / Hormuz Escort Paused / Brent -7.8% $101 / SPX+NQ New ATH
V · TRACK RECORD — 6 Setups Reviewed (No.002 May 1 + No.003 May 4) / 3W 3L / AMD +25.8% +16.9% / BRENT -8.1% Reversal

flagged_setups.thesis: Post-earnings momentum setup: Q1 $10.25B +38% YoY, DC $5.8B +57%, EPS $1.37 vs $1.28 est, Q2 guide $11.2B midpoint crushes $10.52B consensus. Eight analyst firms raised targets. Best post-earnings day since 2019. Stock set new ATH at $421.39. Three-condition entry: DC acceleration confirmed, margin held, guide beat. Momentum continuation into 5-day window. Risk: one-day +18% gains often see 3-5% mean reversion before resuming; any macro shock or NVDA-negative news could drag semis.
flagged_setups.price_note: $421.39 close May 6 2026 — all-time high · StockAnalysis / CNBC / Artvoice May 6 2026

flagged_setups.thesis: Post-earnings setup: Q2 EPS $1.57 beat $1.50 est, Rev $25.2B +7%, SVOD operating margin 10.6% (first double-digit milestone), Experiences record revenue, FY26 adj. EPS guide +16% incl. 53rd week, Q3 total segment OSI guide $5.3B. D'Amaro first earnings beat by $320M — new CEO narrative building. At least $8B share repurchase planned. Entry: $107.42 close May 6 after +6.9% session. Risk: streaming competition intensifies, FX headwinds on international Experiences.
flagged_setups.price_note: $107.42 close May 6 2026 (+6.92%) · Motley Fool / TradingKey / Yahoo Finance May 6 2026

flagged_setups.thesis: Iran peace deal near: US sent one-page MOU via Pakistan intermediaries; Trump paused Project Freedom (Hormuz escort); Iran reviewing and expected to respond Thursday. Brent already -7.8% from $113.54 to $101.27 on May 6. If deal closes and Hormuz reopens, ING forecasts $85-90 range. Supply normalization takes weeks — shipping flows will lag. Short bias at $101.96 May 7 AM. Risk: deal collapses (Trump already threatening renewed strikes), Fujairah damage slower to repair than expected.
flagged_setups.price_note: $101.96 Brent May 7 early AM (+0.68% from $101.27 May 6 close, which was -7.8%) · Trading Economics / CNBC May 7 2026

watchlist_hits.event: Q1 FY2026 earnings AH May 5: Rev $10.25B +38% vs $9.89B est; EPS $1.37 vs $1.28 est; DC $5.8B +57%; Q2 guide $11.2B vs $10.52B est; stock +18.6% to $421.39 ATH on May 6
Corning optical fiber partnership for AI infrastructure announced May 6; read-across from AMD DC beat; underperformed vs AMD on the session
Valuation compression despite Q1 beat ($1.63B +85%, EPS $0.33). Post-earnings AH of +0.84% reversed into full session decline. Pattern: Q4 repeat of post-beat selloff on ~200x fwd PE.
AI semis rally companion to AMD; custom silicon vs merchant silicon narrative ongoing; ARM DC royalty doubling is a read-across for AI accelerator demand

tase_events.event: Iran peace deal near — material positive for Israeli security environment; TASE energy importers benefit from Brent -7.8%; defense sector (Elbit/Rafael) may see reduced urgency on US supplemental arms packages if deal materializes

narrative_threads: Hormuz resolution in motion: US-Iran MOU via Pakistan; Brent -7.8% to $101 — peace dividend priced in partially; full Hormuz + Fujairah normalization is weeks-long process; deal not yet signed

AMD vs NVDA divergence: AMD set new ATH (+18.6%) while NVDA gained only +5.4% and remains below Apr 27 ATH — merchant vs custom silicon narrative, export controls, hyperscaler in-house competition all weigh on NVDA specifically

PLTR valuation ceiling: Two consecutive post-earnings selloffs despite fundamental beats (74%→85% revenue acceleration); ~200x fwd PE creates structural resistance at $147-150; institutional selling pattern consistent

Warsh transition T-8 days: Full Senate vote wk May 11; Powell exits May 15; Warsh inherits Brent collapse (removes oil inflation impulse) but PCE +4.5%, ISM Prices 84.6% still structural; labor still tight (ADP +109K, claims at 57-year lows)
Streaming profitability moment: DIS SVOD 10.6% margin is a sector milestone; validates Netflix's margin path and pressure on PARA/WBD to accelerate. D'Amaro era begins with a beat.

today_calendar.event: Initial Jobless Claims (wk ending May 2; prev 189K; cons 205K)

Kevin Warsh full Senate confirmation vote (expected); Brent response to Iran deal outcome
Powell's final day; Warsh takes over as Fed Chair
